Turn 11 ‚ÄĒ degree 16 produces a codimension-one high-torsor obstruction candidate I used only the three files attached to this message. The original target is still the over-G isomorphism question for őďS,őďH, with canonical projections to G, as stated in the problem file.The prior history reduced the surviving test to the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q,X=1+A,Y=1+B,with target X2=1+A2. Turn 9 cleared the residual congruence through I16, and Turn 10 isolated degree 16 as the first genuinely nonlinear 8+8 layer.

The new computation is:The¬†Turn-10¬†high-correction¬†torsor¬†does¬†not¬†clear¬†degree¬†16.

More precisely, the quadratic affine mapőė16:kerD‚Č§15‚ü∂L16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2]has image of rank 35, not 36. The actual degree-16 square class lies outside that image.I rebuilt the degree-16 calculation in the same restricted Magnus model used in the previous turns: L‚ąô‚äāF2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü© is the free restricted Lie algebra, actual group representatives are generated recursively by commutators and squares, and the degree-n boundary is‚ąān:Ln‚äēLn‚ÜíLn+1,(W,Q)‚Ü¶[A,W]+[B,Q],with [U,V]=UV+VU. This matches the Turn-10 setup, wheredimL8=36,dimL16=4116,rank‚ąā15=4080,so the degree-16 quotient is the 36-dimensional square quotientL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2].

For the Turn-10 high-correction spaceV‚Č§14=d=8‚®Ā14(Ld‚äēLd),with the two copies placed on Y1 and the section twist q, I recovered exactly the structural numbers from Turn 10:dimV‚Č§14=5076,rankD‚Č§15=4684,dimkerD‚Č§15=392.

Then I computed the full degree-16 torsor actionőė16 (k)=‚Ąď16 (k)+Q8,8(k),where ‚Ąď16 is the forced Bockstein contribution of the actual restricted representatives, and Q8,8 is the quadratic Maurer‚ÄďCartan contribution from pairs of degree-8 corrections. This was exactly the test Turn 10 left open.

The result is:rank‚ü®őė16 (kerD‚Č§15)‚ü©=35.

The polarization of Q8,8 adds no missing direction: the affine/quadratic image still lies in a single hyperplane of L8[2].There is a clean way to name the missing functional. In the recursive restricted L8 -basis generated by [A,‚ąí], [B,‚ąí], and restricted squares, the codimension-one functional may be chosen as őĽ=¬†‚Äúcoefficient¬†of¬†((AB+BA)4)[2]plus¬†coefficient¬†of¬†(B8)[2].‚ÄĚ Equivalently, őĽ extracts the parity of the two square coordinates ((AB+BA)4)2 and(B8)2=B16 insideL16/im‚ąā15‚ČÖL8[2].

The computation givesőĽ(őė16 (k))=0for¬†every¬†k‚ąąkerD‚Č§15.Next I solved the lower equations through degrees 9,‚Ä¶,15 for the actual residual tail of the Turn-5/Turn-6 I9 -base lift. The degree-16 square class ő©16 of that solved lift satisfiesőĽ(ő©16)=1.

Thereforeő©16‚ąą/imőė16.So, within the Turn-10 torsor, degree 16 is not another false fixed-gauge tail. It survives the full 392-dimensional invisible high-correction family.I also ran the obvious structural sanity check, because a codimension-one failure could otherwise be an artifact of placing corrections only on Y1 and q. Enlarging the high-correction space to allow a third copy placed on Y0 givesdimV‚Č§14(3)=7614,rankD‚Č§15(3)=4684,dimkerD‚Č§15(3)=2930.Even in this enlarged high-correction kernel, the degree-16 square-moving image still has rank 35, with the same missing functional őĽ, and the actual degree-16 class still has őĽ=1. Thus the failure is not caused by the narrow Y1/q placement convention.I am not yet calling this a proof of non-isomorphism. The remaining subtlety is lower-base freedom. Turn 5 already emphasized that degree 8 was not a single-tail problem: lower Frattini section choices could move an apparent obstruction.

The present computation fixes the I9 -base lift from the previous sweep and then varies all high corrections of initial degree at least 8. To promote the degree-16 class to a genuine over-G obstruction, one still has to prove that earlier I9 -base choices of degrees 4,5,6,7 cannot change őĽ through interactions such as 4+12, 5+11, 6+10, 7+9, or 4+4+8.

So the status is now sharper:degree¬†16¬†survives¬†the¬†full¬†high-correction¬†torsor,¬†but¬†not¬†yet¬†the¬†full¬†lower-base¬†torsor.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn-10 map őė16:kerD‚Č§15‚ÜíL8[2]is not surjective; its image has rank 35. The actual degree-16 square class has nonzero value under a functional őĽ annihilating that image. This remains true even after adding high corrections on Y0.

GAP: This is still not a complete disproof of őďS‚ČÖGőďH, because the degree-16 class has not yet been tested against the full freedom of lower I9 -base choices from degrees 4 through 7.

NEXT: Build the full degree-16 torsor including lower I9 -base deformations. Track only the scalar functional őĽ. If every lower-base deformation also has őĽ=0, then őĽ(ő©16)=1 is a genuine obstruction and gives őďSÓÄ†‚ČÖGőďH. If some lower-base deformation changes őĽ, then degree 16 dies after all.[DEG16-CODIM1-HIGH-TORSOR-OBSTRUCTION]
